title: Eusteralis yatabeana
taxonomy: Eusteralis yatabeana is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. It is native to Australia, specifically in the states of New South Wales and Victoria. The plant is a type of tree or shrub that belongs to the genus Eusteralis. The classification of Eusteralis yatabeana includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Magnoliophyta. Further classification places it in the class Magnoliopsida, order Oxalidales, and family Cunoniaceae.

intro: Eusteralis yatabeana, also known as Eusteralis or Japanese angelica, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, belonging to the family Apiaceae. Traditionally, Eusteralis yatabeana has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Eusteralis yatabeana include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Eusteralis yatabeana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into soups and other culinary dishes.
